task: Gate 3 with complete graph — GNN trained on gate2 data, tested on gate3 lemma-novelty

prior_baseline.note: Prior Gate 3 result with partial graph (Algebra subgraph ~10K nodes)

results_full_graph.note: 116K candidates too many for GNN to discriminate; all 14 failed

results_algebra_subgraph.failed_reason: Theorems require specific lemma retrieval (derivative_X_pow, natDegree_C_mul_X_pow, degree_add_eq_left_of_degree_lt, etc.) that the GNN cannot retrieve from 3840 candidates.

shape_matcher.note: Shape-matcher rate (43%) exceeds 5% bound. Gate3 polynomial theorems are simpler than physics post1905 theorems — many are solved by `simp` alone because Mathlib4's simp set includes eval_add, eval_C, eval_X.

comparison.verdict: GNN 28.6% beats prior 21.4% (+7.2pp) and beats heuristics 21.4% (+7.2pp). But 28.6% < 45% MARGINAL threshold. Gate 3 NOT PASSED.

analysis.limiting_factor: Lemma retrieval for polynomial-specific lemmas (derivative, degree, monic)
analysis.all_proofs_are_simp: All successful proofs use `simp` — GNN correctly selects it when appropriate
analysis.full_graph_too_large: 116K candidates degrades to 0%. Need better embeddings or candidate pre-filtering
analysis.algebra_subgraph_works: 3840 nodes is tractable — embeddings provide useful signal
analysis.fine_tuning_helped: GoalEncoder SFT on gate2 boosted val top-1 from ~4.4% to 8.3%
analysis.path_forward: Need stronger lemma retrieval: larger GNN (5-50M params), better pretraining, or candidate pre-filtering beyond domain subgraph
